_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize Hays County TX local batch: county judge (Becerra, LOST 2026 runoff), commissioners Pct1/2/3 (Ingalsbe, Gutierrez Cohen [now D nominee for County Judge], Hammer [lone R]), and Criminal DA (Higgins, lame-duck not running 2026). Local tier, evidence-only, every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

ruben-becerra.notes_set: Hays County Judge (sitting since 2019). CANDIDACY: LOST the 2026 Democratic primary runoff (May 26, 2026) to Commissioner Michelle Gutierrez Cohen, 58.3%-41.7%; not on the November 2026 ballot, serves out term through Dec 2026. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (local tier). Documented contrary record: authored/championed county LGBTQIA+ Pride proclamations (2023, 2026); campaign lists 'protection of reproductive rights'; voted FY2025 13.8% property-tax-rate increase ('tiny uptick'); FY2021 proposed budget cut Sheriff's Office ~4% below request amid organized law-enforcement opposition (he denied 'defunding' but enacted cuts); as County Judge imposed COVID stay-at-home order/curfew (Mar 2020) and countywide mask mandate (Jun 2020) and called Abbott's lifting of the state mask mandate 'premature and reckless'; called an April 2025 ICE operation 'kidnappings' and told local law enforcement not to cooperate with ICE.

0.note: Campaign/bio lists 'protection of civil and reproductive rights' among governing principles; no affirmation of personhood from conception.

1.note: Self-stated support for reproductive rights is the opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

0.note: Authored/championed county LGBTQIA+ Pride Month proclamations celebrating 'the full diversity of our region' (2023, 2026) — contrary to affirming exclusive man-woman marriage.

2.note: Authored LGBTQIA+ (trans-inclusive) Pride proclamation; no affirmation of immutable biological sex.

4.note: Authored Pride proclamations actively promoting LGBTQ identity in public policy.

2.note: Voted (Sept 17, 2024) for a 13.8% property-tax-rate increase to $0.35/$100, calling it a 'tiny uptick' — expansionary tax/spend posture, not fiscal restraint.

0.note: FY2021 proposed budget cut Sheriff's Office ~4% (15.6% below request), zeroed 48 replacement vehicles, cut Constable budgets, drawing organized law-enforcement opposition; no affirmative pro-police advocacy. Does not meet the high bar for TRUE; documented cuts make it FALSE.

2.note: Imposed countywide COVID mask mandate (Jun 2020) and stay-at-home/curfew (Mar 2020); criticized the state for LIFTING its mask mandate — imposed mandates rather than protecting citizens from them.

1.note: Called an April 2025 ICE operation 'kidnappings'; opposes immigration enforcement — contrary to supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: Told local law enforcement not to cooperate with ICE ('I do not want our local law enforcement acting like ICE agents') — contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

debbie-gonzales-ingalsbe.notes_set: Hays County Commissioner, Precinct 1 (D), in office since 1996; reelected 2024 (59.6% primary), term 2024-2028, next election 2028. Former Deputy Constable. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (local tier). Documented: Feb-2024 candidate questionnaire opposing pro-life local ordinances and affirming healthcare/abortion access; sat on the Commissioners Court that unanimously adopted annual LGBTQIA+ Pride proclamations (2023-2026, no recorded dissent); voted for FY2025 (5-0) and FY2026 (4-1, yes) property-tax-rate increases. PRO-LAW-ENFORCEMENT CREDIT: as a former Deputy Constable she affirmatively pushed in FY2026 budget talks for 'more sheriff support, more patrol vehicle support' and co-chairs the county Criminal Justice Coordinating Commission / championed the Jail-Based Competency Restoration Program — affirmative advocacy beyond routine budget voting. Voted 5-0 for a June-2025 resolution constraining immigration-enforcement conduct (pro-immigrant posture).

0.note: Feb-2024 questionnaire: 'I have no interest and will not support local policies or ordinances that intentionally target women and pregnant individuals' and would 'never intentionally or overtly hinder anyone's access to healthcare' — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-access framing is the opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

4.note: Sitting member of the Commissioners Court that unanimously adopted annual LGBTQIA+ Pride Month proclamations (2023-2026) with no recorded dissent — concurred in public-policy promotion of LGBTQ identity.

2.note: Voted for the FY2025 (5-0, ~13.8% rate hike) and FY2026 (4-1, voted yes) property-tax-rate increases and expanded spending — expansionary posture, not fiscal restraint.

0.note: Former Deputy Constable who affirmatively advocated in FY2026 budget talks for 'more sheriff support, more patrol vehicle support and other public safety resources' — affirmative pro-law-enforcement advocacy beyond routine budget voting (credit where earned).

1.note: Voted 5-0 for a June-2025 resolution affirming humane treatment of immigrants in custody and demanding due process / enforcement transparency — contrary to supporting mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

2.note: Pro-immigrant resolution constraining enforcement conduct — contrary to opposing sanctuary-style policy.

michelle-gutierrez-cohen.notes_set: Hays County Commissioner, Precinct 2 (D), elected 2022 (60.85%). CANDIDACY: now the Democratic NOMINEE for Hays County Judge — won the May 26, 2026 primary runoff over incumbent Ruben Becerra (58.3%), faces Republican Geoffrey Tahuahua in the Nov 2026 general; holds the commissioner seat through end of term. Founder of Hays Latinos United (COVID resource group). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (local tier). Documented: publicly championed county Pride Month proclamations (2023; June 2024 said 'love is love' and 'I will always advocate for marginalized communities'); part of the unanimous FY2025 13.8% tax-rate-increase vote and champion of the $440M road bond (debt); voted for the June-2025 humane-treatment-of-immigrants resolution and is the founder of a Hispanic-community advocacy group (pro-immigrant posture); COVID public-health work (mobile vaccine clinic).

0.note: On the June-2024 Pride proclamation said 'I will always advocate for marginalized communities in Hays County and I believe love is love' — contrary to affirming exclusive man-woman marriage.

4.note: Publicly championed county LGBTQIA+ Pride proclamations (2023, 2024) — active promotion of LGBTQ identity in public policy.

2.note: Part of the unanimous FY2025 13.8% property-tax-rate increase and champion of the $440M road bond (county debt) — expansionary tax/debt posture, not fiscal restraint.

1.note: Voted for the June-2025 humane-treatment-of-immigrants resolution; founder of Hays Latinos United pro-immigrant advocacy — contrary to supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: Consistent pro-immigrant posture; no support for ICE-cooperation/anti-sanctuary policy — contrary to opposing sanctuary policy.

morgan-hammer.notes_set: Hays County Commissioner, Precinct 3 (R), elected Nov 2024 (52.82%), term to 2028. Board member, Hays County Republican Women; active at First Baptist San Marcos. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (local tier). DEVIATION FLAG (lone Republican, squishy on the social axis): in her OWN words (June 9, 2026) she affirmatively advocated FOR the county LGBTQIA+ Pride Month proclamation ('the LGB community went through some serious times of hardship'). CONSERVATIVE CREDIT: cast the lone dissenting vote (4-1, Sept 16, 2025) against the FY2026 budget/tax-rate increase, wanting to cut new positions to lower taxes (HARD fiscal restraint); endorsed by the Hays County Law Enforcement Association, San Marcos POA, and Sheriff Cutler, and completed the Sheriff's Citizen's Academy (affirmative pro-law-enforcement). Voted 5-0 (with the court) for the June-2025 immigrant due-process/humane-treatment resolution. No documented position on life, family/curriculum, Christian-liberty policy, elections, or guns (personal faith documented but not a policy act).

4.note: In her own words (June 9, 2026) affirmatively advocated for the LGBTQIA+ Pride Month proclamation, citing 'the LGB community went through some serious times of hardship' — active promotion of LGBTQ identity in public policy (deviation flag for an R).

2.note: Cast the lone dissenting vote (4-1, Sept 16, 2025) against the FY2026 budget and $0.3999 tax rate, arguing to cut new positions to lower taxes and protect long-term sustainability — documented fiscal restraint.

0.note: Endorsed by the Hays County Law Enforcement Association, San Marcos Police Officers' Association, and (then-)Sheriff Gary Cutler; completed the Sheriff's Office Citizen's Academy and pledged to work closely with law enforcement — affirmative pro-police record beyond budget voting.

1.note: Voted 5-0 (with the court) for the June-2025 resolution demanding due process and federal-agent identification — constrains/criticizes immigration enforcement, inconsistent with supporting mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

kelly-higgins.notes_set: Hays County Criminal District Attorney (D), elected 2022 (53.06%). CANDIDACY: lame-duck incumbent — did NOT file for re-election; Alfonso Salazar won the March-2026 Democratic primary (54.5%) to succeed him and runs unopposed in November. Higgins serves through end of term (Jan 2027). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (local tier). Documented progressive/rogue-prosecutor record: categorically declines prosecution of simple cannabis possession (campaign promise he says 'will happen'); refuses to use county resources to prosecute abortion ('a matter of conscience for me... I cannot charge medical patients with crimes'), which triggered a Sept-2023 HB17 'rogue prosecutor' removal petition; built diversion / mental-health courts for nonviolent offenders and aligned with decarceration group Mano Amiga. No documented positions on marriage/sexuality, family/curriculum, Christian liberty, budgets, elections, or guns (out of a DA's lane or not stated).

0.note: Refuses to prosecute abortion: 'This is a matter of conscience for me... I cannot charge medical patients with crimes'; won't use county resources to prosecute abortion — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Categorical non-enforcement of pro-life state law (subject of an HB17 removal petition) is the opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

Categorically declines prosecution of simple cannabis possession; self-described reform DA aligned with decarceration advocates (Mano Amiga); diversion-first posture — the rogue-prosecutor / soft-on-crime pattern this cell opposes.
